name: Mountaincrest
description_short: MountainCrest Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Washington members with checking, savings, loans, and credit products.
description_long:
MountainCrest Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union serving members across Washington state, with physical locations in Arlington, Everett, and Marysville. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), it operates under the cooperative credit union model rather than shareholder-driven banking. The organization has been structured to prioritize member benefits and community-focused financial services.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal financial products including free checking accounts, personal savings, youth savings accounts (PAWS Club and Teen accounts), and holiday club accounts. On the lending side, MountainCrest provides auto loans, RV loans, home mortgage loans and refinancing options (including no-cost refinance products), home equity loans, personal loans, personal lines of credit, and signature loans. They also issue a Visa credit card (Visa Platinum) with promotional rates, offer certificates of deposit (CDs), and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). Digital services include online banking with bill pay, mobile banking, person-to-person payments, a digital wallet, and access to surcharge-free ATMs through the CO-OP network and shared branching.

MountainCrest distinguishes itself through member-centric initiatives including scholarship programs for education, volunteer opportunities for governance participation, and financial education resources through Banzai Financial Fitness and identity theft prevention tools. The credit union promotes accessibility with AudioEye-enabled web tools and offers conveniences like online loan applications, multiple payment methods, loan status checking, and skip-a-payment options. Their emphasis on community involvement and member voice in credit union direction reflects traditional credit union values.

As a regional credit union, MountainCrest serves a specific geographic footprint in Washington state. Potential members should verify eligibility requirements for membership, as credit unions typically have membership field-of-membership criteria. The institution appears well-established with standard credit union offerings, though specific information about membership fees, rate competitiveness, or asset size is not disclosed on the public website pages provided.
services: Youth savings accounts (PAWS Club and Teen accounts)
Auto loans and recreational vehicle (RV) loans
Home mortgage loans and refinancing (including no-cost refinance products)
Visa credit cards (Visa Platinum with promotional rates)
Online banking, bill pay, and mobile banking
CO-OP surcharge-free ATM network access and shared branching
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means earnings can be returned to members through better rates and lower fees
Free checking account available with no mentioned monthly maintenance fees
Surcharge-free ATM access through CO-OP network and shared branching at other credit unions nationwide
Comprehensive loan products including specialized options like no-cost home refinance and RV loans
Promotional credit card rate (2.99% APR fixed for 12 months on Visa Platinum purchases and balance transfers)
Online loan applications and multiple convenient payment options including skip-a-payment flexibility
Member engagement opportunities through scholarship programs and board/supervisory committee volunteering
Financial education resources including Banzai Financial Fitness and identity theft prevention tools
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only three branch locations (Arlington, Everett, Marysville) restricts physical access for non-local members
Membership eligibility requirements typical to credit unions may exclude some consumers outside the field-of-membership
No specific APR or rate information disclosed on website for most products, making rate comparison difficult
Limited information about minimum deposit requirements, membership fees, or account opening requirements
No mention of business banking products or services for small business owners
best_for: Washington state residents in the Arlington, Everett, or Marysville areas seeking member-focused banking with local branch access
Borrowers looking for competitive home equity loans, auto loans, or mortgage refinancing with promotional rates
Members seeking surcharge-free ATM access and the ability to conduct transactions at other credit unions nationwide via shared branching
Consumers valuing member governance participation and community-oriented financial institutions
diagnosis: MountainCrest Credit Union is best for Washington-based consumers seeking traditional credit union membership with local branch access, competitive lending rates, and member-owned governance participation. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—non-local members will rely entirely on digital services and the CO-OP ATM/shared branching network, and membership eligibility must be verified based on their field-of-membership criteria.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Mountaincrest — Credit Union in Arlington, WA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Mountaincrest — NCUA-insured credit union in Arlington, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
